codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of per-platform draft persistence, independent checklist state, localized error handling, and next-step navigation affordances. The code follows project architecture conventions — thin route handlers delegating to workspace-store services, domain types from lib/domain/types, and React client-side state management that initializes from persisted data on load. All six validation assertions (VAL-STUDIO-005 through VAL-STUDIO-010) are covered by both implementation and comprehensive tests (36 new tests across 3 files). Platform isolation is correctly enforced: checklist and draft state are keyed per platform, saveStatus resets on tab switch, and error messages are localized. Minor style and resilience nits noted below, none blocking.

issues.description: Duplicate import source: `findItemById` and `updatePlatformDraft` are imported from `@/lib/services/workspace-store` in two separate import statements (lines 6-7). Should be consolidated into a single import statement for cleanliness.

handleNextReadyVideo calls handleSaveDraft() which catches errors internally and sets saveStatus to 'error' without rethrowing. If the auto-save fails, navigation still proceeds via router.push, meaning the user could navigate away from unsaved draft changes without explicit warning. The current draft state is in client memory so it won't be lost immediately, but the expectation of 'save before navigating' is not fully guaranteed on save failure.

The checklist items (cover-uploaded, tags-added, ready-to-publish) are hardcoded in the JSX template. While the API and persistence layer support arbitrary checklist keys, the UI renders only these three fixed items. This is acceptable for V1 but limits future per-platform checklist customization without a UI change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The drafts route uses two separate import lines for the same module (`@/lib/services/workspace-store`). AGENTS.md doesn't mention an import consolidation convention, but all other route files in the codebase use single import statements per module. This is a very minor style inconsistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/api/items/[itemId]/drafts/route.ts lines 6-7: two import statements from '@/lib/services/workspace-store'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the fullstack-web-worker procedure correctly: TDD with tests first, verification before completion, all validators run. skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations. The skill file is accurate to the worker's actual workflow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows all verification commands passed (tests, typecheck, lint). Transcript skeleton confirms TDD approach.

summary: Pass. The feature correctly implements per-platform draft persistence, independent checklist state, sparse-content manual editing, localized platform error handling, and Next Platform / Next Ready Video navigation affordances. All six validation assertions (VAL-STUDIO-005 through 010) are fulfilled. The code respects the project's architectural layering (thin route handlers, service-layer persistence, domain types). Three non-blocking issues noted: a duplicate import, a minor auto-save-before-navigation resilience gap, and hardcoded checklist items in the UI template. Test coverage is comprehensive with 36 new tests across API, editor UI, and navigation concerns.
